daily_brief: The Middle East war dominated global developments Thursday as Iran struck Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG facility—the world's largest—causing extensive damage and sending oil prices surging [Financial Times, Al Jazeera]. The attack came after Israel hit Iran's South Pars gas field, with Tehran also acknowledging the death of security chief Ali Larijani in an Israeli strike [RFI]. US intelligence officials testified that Iran's regime remains 'intact but largely degraded,' while repeatedly failing to substantiate the Trump administration's claims about imminent threats that justified the war [BBC, Globe and Mail, RNZ]. In Europe, leaders convene in Brussels Thursday facing twin crises: unlocking a €90 billion Ukraine loan blocked by Hungary's Viktor Orban, and confronting a new energy shock as the conflict disrupts supplies through the Strait of Hormuz [France 24, BBC]. Ukraine's President Zelensky warned the BBC that the war in Iran would create missile deficits for his country as US resources shift elsewhere. Meanwhile, Costa Rica severed diplomatic ties with Cuba, with President Chaves declaring the 'hemisphere must be cleansed of communists' [Le Monde].

top_stories.headline: Iran Strikes Qatar's Major LNG Facility in Escalating Gulf Energy War
bullets: Iran launched missile attacks on Qatar's Ras Laffan facility—site of the world's largest LNG operations—causing fires and 'extensive damage' according to Qatari officials
Qatar ordered Iran's military and security attachés to leave within 24 hours following the attack
The strikes came in retaliation for Israeli attacks on Iran's South Pars gas field; Trump threatened to 'blow up' a major Iranian gas field if attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure continue
Oil and gas prices surged globally as the conflict threatens critical energy supply chains

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Led with economic impact and market implications

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized diplomatic fallout and regional escalation

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Focused on Gulf state responses and Iranian justifications

top_stories.headline: US Intelligence Officials Fail to Back Trump's Iran War Justifications in Senate Testimony
bullets: DNI Tulsi Gabbard told senators Iran's regime is 'intact but largely degraded' but repeatedly declined to confirm whether Iran posed an imminent threat before the war began
Gabbard stated only the president can determine imminent threats, prompting sharp pushback from Democratic senators
Officials contradicted or failed to substantiate multiple Trump administration claims about the war, including assertions about Iran rebuilding its nuclear program
Testimony came one day after National Counterterrorism Center director Joe Kent resigned, suggesting the administration lied about imminent threats

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Straight reporting on testimony, noted Kent resignation context

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized officials' evasiveness and contradictions with administration claims

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Analysis focused on credibility gap between officials' testimony and Trump's statements

top_stories.headline: EU Summit Tackles €90 Billion Ukraine Loan Blocked by Orban Amid New Energy Crisis
bullets: European leaders meet Thursday in Brussels to unlock €90 billion loan for Ukraine, stalled by Hungarian PM Viktor Orban over damaged Druzhba pipeline
Ukraine accepted EU technical and financial assistance to repair the pipeline, which carries Russian crude to Hungary and Slovakia
The summit agenda has been overtaken by Europe's new energy shock from the Middle East war, with diplomats expressing frustration at repeating past vulnerabilities
Orban linked pipeline repairs to loan approval ahead of Hungary's April 12 elections, with Zelensky calling it 'blackmail'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Orban's electoral motivations and 'no oil, no money' stance

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Broader context on Europe's recurring energy vulnerabilities

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Focused on diplomatic breakthrough over pipeline repairs

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Framed as Middle East war 'gatecrashing' EU economy summit

top_stories.headline: Iran Confirms Death of Security Chief Ali Larijani in Israeli Strike, Launches Retaliatory Missiles
bullets: Iran acknowledged the death of Ali Larijani, 67, secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, in an Israeli strike Tuesday—the most prominent Iranian figure killed since Khamenei's death on February 28
Iranian Revolutionary Guards launched missiles at central Israel in immediate retaliation, killing two people near Tel Aviv
Funerals held Wednesday for Larijani and Basij paramilitary force head Gholamreza Soleimani, also killed in separate Israeli strike
Iran has executed three people convicted of killing police during January unrest, accused of acting on behalf of US and Israel

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting on Iranian statements and retaliatory strikes

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Included context on domestic executions amid wartime tensions

top_stories.headline: Zelensky Warns Ukraine Faces Missile Shortage as Middle East War Diverts US Resources
bullets: In BBC interview, Ukrainian President Zelensky said the Iran war would create a 'deficit' of missiles for Ukraine as US resources are redirected
Zelensky claimed Putin wants a 'long war' between US, Israel and Iran because it weakens Ukraine through depleted reserves and higher energy prices
Ukrainian president said Trump is not on 'any side' in the Russia-Ukraine war and does not want to 'irritate' Putin
Zelensky urged Trump and UK PM Starmer to meet and find common ground despite Trump's criticism of Starmer

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Exclusive interview framing; emphasized Zelensky's 'very bad feeling' about war's impact
